mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where unemployment rises above the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to yield positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration coincides with negative 5-day returns in > 60% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, monetary policy restricts credit availability and raises discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in > 70% of periods where the federal funds rate remains above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of payroll growth and falling unemployment yields negative 20-day returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side disruption and weakening economic momentum, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops exceeding 1.0% are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances.
